title_zh: 利用引导生成模型估计极端事件概率
title_en: Extreme Event Likelihoods with Guided Generative Models
summary_zh: 在科学、工程和金融领域，许多重要风险来自低概率但高影响的极端事件。本文介绍了使用引导生成模型来估计这些事件的发生概率。
summary_en: Across science, engineering, and finance, many critical risks stem from low-likelihood, high-impact events. This article explores using guided generative models to estimate the probabilities of such events.
why_it_matters_zh: 准确评估极端事件概率有助于风险管理和应对策略制定，推动相关领域的安全性与稳健性提升。
why_it_matters_en: Accurately estimating extreme event probabilities aids in risk management and strategy formulation, enhancing safety and robustness in various fields.

title_zh: StereoSplat+：结合扩散辅助渐进推理的前馈立体高斯散点法
title_en: StereoSplat+: Feed-Forward Stereo Gaussian Splatting with Diffusion-Assisted Progressive Inference
summary_zh: 三维高斯散点法（3DGS）的最新进展实现了高质量的场景表示，用于新视角合成。但现有方法多依赖多视角观测或未来帧信息，这在机器人和增强现实等仅有单一立体相机的场景中难以实现。本文提出了StereoSplat+，一种扩散增强的前馈框架，可从单对立体图像因果重建高质量3DGS场景。方法包括输入不变的StereoSplat网络，通过成本体积分支和三平面三维体积分支融合几何信息，并利用连续位姿编码适配不同视角和相机配置；以及一种称为StereoSplat+的单次扩散渐进推理策略，通过从单一立体对预测3DGS，渲染新视图，使用一步扩散增强器细化，再反馈更新3DGS。KITTI-360数据集实验表明，本方法在遮挡区域和视角外推时，显著提升了新视角渲染质量和几何准确性，优于现有前馈3DGS方法。
summary_en: Recent advances in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) enable high-quality scene representations for novel-view synthesis, but typically require multi-view or future-frame data, which is unavailable in on-device robotics and AR with only a single stereo rig. StereoSplat+ is a diffusion-enhanced feed-forward framework that reconstructs high-quality 3DGS scenes causally from one stereo pair. It employs StereoSplat, an input-invariant estimator that fuses complementary geometry cues via a cost-volume and triplane 3D volume branches with continuous pose encoding to generalize to varying views and camera setups. Then, StereoSplat+ performs diffusion-enhanced one-shot progressive inference: from one stereo pair, it renders novel stereo views, refines them with a one-step diffusion enhancer, and feeds them back to update 3DGS. Experiments on KITTI-360 show improved novel-view rendering quality and geometry accuracy, especially in occlusions and strong view extrapolation, outperforming recent feed-forward 3DGS baselines.
why_it_matters_zh: StereoSplat+解决了受限于单个立体视角的高质量3D场景重建难题，适用于机器人和增强现实等实际应用场景。其创新结合了前馈3DGS网络与扩散模型逐步精化策略，突破了以往依赖多视角数据的限制，提高了遮挡区域和视角外推的表现。
why_it_matters_en: StereoSplat+ addresses the challenge of high-quality 3D scene reconstruction from limited single stereo views, relevant for robotics and AR applications. Its innovation lies in combining a feed-forward 3DGS network with diffusion-assisted progressive refinement, overcoming previous multi-view data reliance and enhancing performance in occluded regions and strong viewpoint extrapolations.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: StereoSplat+: Feed-Forward Stereo Gaussian Splatting with Diffusion-Assisted Progressive Inference. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 探针混合：通过探针学习多模态大语言模型中的特权模态
title_en: Mixture of Probes: Learning from Privileged Modalities in Multimodal LLMs Through Probing
summary_zh: 多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）通常假设训练和推理时均可获得所有模态数据，但现实中常常只有训练阶段可获得辅助模态。本文提出了探针混合（MoP）框架，通过结构化探针机制区分模态特异和模态通用信号，保留模态依赖结构的同时实现跨模态迁移表达。结合探针解耦损失的MoP跨模态训练策略（MoP-X），在八个任务和四种模态的综合评测中，MoP在推理阶段仅用单一模态输入时，较强基线最高提升65%，显示辅助模态即使不可用于推理也可显著提升模型性能。代码、模型和评测协议将开源。
summary_en: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) generally assume all training modalities are available at inference, which is often violated in real scenarios where auxiliary modalities exist only during training. This paper introduces Mixture of Probes (MoP), which disentangles modality-specific and modality-general signals via a structured probing mechanism extracting information from intermediate representations of a shared encoder. The MoP Cross-modal Training (MoP-X) strategy with a probe disentanglement loss prevents collapse and fosters cross-modal learning. Evaluated on eight tasks across four modalities under a protocol treating each modality independently at inference, MoP outperforms strong baselines with up to 65% relative gain, demonstrating effective use of privileged modalities during training even if unavailable at inference. Code and models will be made public.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究创新性地解决了训练阶段多模态、推理阶段单模态的不匹配问题，通过设计探针结构有效提取并解耦模态信息，提升了模型在现实应用中缺乏某些模态输入时的表现，拓展了多模态学习的应用边界。
why_it_matters_en: This work innovatively addresses the mismatch between multi-modal training and single-modal inference by designing structured probes that effectively extract and disentangle modality information, substantially improving model performance when some modalities are absent at inference and thereby broadening the practical applicability of multimodal learning.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Mixture of Probes: Learning from Privileged Modalities in Multimodal LLMs Through Probing. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 72.

title_zh: 安全伪装：医疗图像保密建模的图像伪装系统评估
title_en: Secure-by-Disguise: A Systematic Evaluation of Image Disguising for Confidential Medical Image Modeling
summary_zh: 云端深度学习推动了大规模医疗图像分析，但将敏感患者图像外包进行模型开发带来了重大隐私问题。图像伪装作为一种新兴的隐私增强技术，通过将图像转换为视觉上难以理解的形式，同时保留用于后续学习的信息。我们建立了统一框架，评估了代表性方法DisguisedNets和NeuraCrypt，在涉及分类和语义分割的四个数据集上，分析它们的预测效用、效率和对重建攻击的鲁棒性。结果显示，图像伪装对不同任务的表现差异显著：对医疗图像分类保持良好效用，但对密集语义分割产生较大性能下降。随机多维变换（RMT）在性能和安全性间提供最佳平衡，而基于AES的伪装严重影响效用。此外，基于回归的重建攻击在自然图像上的有效性，在真实医疗图像上则较低。此研究系统评估了隐私增强技术在医疗AI保密应用中的适用性。
summary_en: Cloud-based deep learning enables large-scale medical image analysis but poses significant privacy risks when sensitive patient images are outsourced for model development. Image disguising has emerged as a promising privacy-enhancing technology (PET) that transforms images into visually unintelligible forms while preserving information for downstream learning. We established a unified framework to evaluate representative methods, DisguisedNets and NeuraCrypt, across four datasets involving classification and semantic segmentation tasks. Our analysis assessed predictive utility, efficiency, and robustness against reconstruction attacks. Results showed image disguising performance varies by task: methods preserved utility for medical image classification but caused substantial degradation in dense semantic segmentation. Specifically, Randomized Multidimensional Transformation (RMT) offered the best balance of performance and security, whereas AES-based disguising severely impacted utility. Regression-based reconstruction attacks effective on natural images were considerably less successful on realistic medical images. These findings provide a systematic assessment of PET suitability for confidential medical AI applications.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究填补了医疗图像隐私保护技术系统性评估的空白，明确不同图像伪装方法在多任务上的适用性和局限，为安全利用云端AI模型开发提供了重要参考，推动医疗AI隐私保护技术的应用和发展。
why_it_matters_en: This study fills a gap in systematic evaluation of privacy-enhancing technologies for medical images, clarifying the applicability and limitations of different image disguising methods across tasks. It offers critical guidance for securely leveraging cloud AI model development and advances privacy protection in medical AI.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Secure-by-Disguise: A Systematic Evaluation of Image Disguising for Confidential Medical Image Modeling. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 空间可控的多视角室内场景重光照的解耦照明先验
title_en: Decoupled Illumination Priors for Spatially Controllable Multi-View Indoor Scene Relighting
summary_zh: 室内场景重光照需要实现逼真的视觉效果、精确的空间控制以及严格的多视角一致性。虽然基于扩散的图像编辑模型通过文本提示实现语义光照操作，但精确的3D光源定位往往会破坏其生成先验。本文提出了Lume-Palette，一个利用语义照明先验实现空间可控多视角室内重光照的渐进框架。该方法将重光照分为两个阶段：(1) 照明蒸馏，从预训练扩散模型提取标准照明调色板以保持真实的材质-光线交互；(2) 照明投射，将目标空间光照条件显式映射至稀疏3D几何。为高效处理密集多视角多模态输入，设计了非对称多视角条件策略，选择性注入关键空间上下文。实验证明，Lume-Palette在多种合成和真实场景中表现出逼真、空间可控且多视角一致的重光照效果。项目页面：this https URL
summary_en: Indoor scene relighting requires photorealism, precise spatial control, and strict multi-view consistency. Although diffusion-based image editing models enable semantic lighting manipulation via text prompts, enforcing exact 3D light placement tends to disrupt their generative priors. We propose Lume-Palette, a progressive framework leveraging semantic lighting priors for spatially controllable multi-view indoor relighting. The method decouples relighting into two stages: (1) illumination distillation, extracting canonical illumination palettes from a pretrained diffusion model to preserve realistic material-light interactions; (2) illumination casting, explicitly mapping target spatial lighting conditions defined from sparse 3D geometry. To efficiently handle dense multi-view and multi-modal inputs, an asymmetric multi-view conditioning strategy selectively injects essential spatial context. Experiments on diverse synthetic and real-world scenes demonstrate that Lume-Palette delivers photorealistic, spatially controllable, and multi-view consistent relighting results. Project page: this https URL
why_it_matters_zh: Lume-Palette通过解耦照明先验与空间映射，突破了扩散模型难以精确控制3D光源位置的技术瓶颈，实现了高质量、多视角一致且空间可控的室内重光照，推动了图像生成与计算机视觉在复杂光照场景下的结合与应用。
why_it_matters_en: By decoupling illumination priors and spatial mapping, Lume-Palette overcomes the diffusion model's challenge in precise 3D light placement, achieving high-quality, multi-view consistent, and spatially controllable indoor relighting, advancing integration of image generation and computer vision in complex lighting scenarios.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Decoupled Illumination Priors for Spatially Controllable Multi-View Indoor Scene Relighting. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 通过格遍历对多层感知机的区间认证
title_en: Interval Certifications for Multilayered Perceptrons via Lattice Traversal
summary_zh: 本文提出了一个严谨的理论框架，针对AI安全中的基础问题——对抗鲁棒性。研究表明，对抗鲁棒性问题可以归约为一个格遍历问题。该格的每个元素对应一个包含输入点的区间（轴对齐的超矩形）。对于多层感知机(MLP)分类器，区间若保证任意在区间内的输入扰动均不改变MLP的预测，则为一个可靠认证；若输入一旦离开区间预测必变，则为完整认证。本文开发了格遍历算子，并采用“细化与验证”的迭代方案，用形式化验证器保证可靠认证的最大性和完整认证的最小性。此外，针对完整认证的最小解可在多项式时间内通过oracle调用获得，而可靠认证则存在强不可解问题。还研究了对称区间的优化算法，并通过新系统ParallelepipedoNN进行了实证评估。
summary_en: This work presents a rigorous theoretical framework addressing adversarial robustness in AI safety. It reduces the adversarial robustness problem to a lattice traversal problem, where each lattice element represents an interval (axis-aligned hyper-rectangle) containing an input point. For multilayer perceptron (MLP) classifiers, an interval is a sound certification if perturbations inside it do not change the MLP's prediction, and a complete certification if moving outside guarantees a prediction change. The authors develop lattice traversal operators within a refine & verify iterative scheme, ensuring sound maximality and complete minimality using formal MLP verifiers. They reveal that complete certification's minimum solution is obtainable in polynomial oracle calls, unlike sound certification which is strongly intractable. Algorithms for optimization in symmetric intervals are also provided, accompanied by empirical evaluation using the ParallelepipedoNN system.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究首次系统地将对抗鲁棒性认证问题转化为格遍历问题，提出了完整认证的新概念并解决了相应的计算难题。这不仅填补了理论空白，还为安全可靠的神经网络设计提供了数学基础，推动AI安全领域的发展。
why_it_matters_en: This work systematically reduces adversarial robustness certification to lattice traversal and introduces the novel concept of complete certification, addressing its computational challenges. It fills a theoretical gap and lays a mathematical foundation for designing safe and reliable neural networks, advancing AI safety research.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Interval Certifications for Multilayered Perceptrons via Lattice Traversal. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 67.

title_zh: 神经代理控制：基于深度学习的大型语言模型驱动的安全控制框架
title_en: Neuro-Agentic Control: A Deep Learning-based LLM-Powered Agentic AI Framework for Controlling Security Controls
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种神经代理控制框架，该框架结合了基于大型语言模型（如Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite）的规划器和预训练的时间序列基础模型（TimesFM），实现物理学基础的自主防御。通过“反事实物理注入”机制，在基础模型的潜在数值空间中模拟大型语言模型建议的干预措施，防止幻觉或不安全操作。该框架在工业数据集（如安全水处理SWaT）上的随机攻击场景评估中，显著优于LSTM和TCN基线，成功避免了33.3%的安全漏洞，且无物理无效（幻觉）行为执行，展现了基础模型作为AI安全“守望者”的有效性。
summary_en: This paper introduces a neuro-agentic control framework that couples an LLM-based planner (e.g., Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite) with a pre-trained Time-Series Foundation Model (TimesFM) to achieve physics-grounded autonomous defense. The 'Counterfactual Physics Injection' mechanism simulates the impact of LLM-proposed interventions within the foundation model’s latent space, enabling rejection of hallucinatory or unsafe actions. Evaluated on an industrial dataset (Secure Water Treatment SWaT) under stochastic attack scenarios, the framework outperformed LSTM and TCN baselines, preventing 33.3% of breaches versus 26.7% and 13.3% respectively, with zero physically invalid actions executed. This demonstrates the effectiveness of foundation models as deterministic sentinels safeguarding agentic AI in critical infrastructure.
why_it_matters_zh: 面对工业物联网环境中传统规则监控的局限及大型语言模型在闭环控制中幻觉问题，此框架创新性地结合深度学习模型和物理仿真机制，提升了网络安全的自主响应能力和安全性，适用于关键基础设施的防御。
why_it_matters_en: Given the limitations of traditional rule-based monitoring in industrial IoT and the hallucination risks of LLMs in closed-loop control, this framework innovatively combines deep learning models with physical simulation mechanisms to enhance autonomous cyber defense and safety, making it suitable for safeguarding critical infrastructure.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Neuro-Agentic Control: A Deep Learning-based LLM-Powered Agentic AI Framework for Controlling Security Controls. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: L-MAD：法律推理中多智能体辩论结构的系统评估
title_en: L-MAD: A Systematic Evaluation of Multi-Agent Debate Structures in Legal Reasoning
summary_zh: 本文介绍了法律多智能体辩论（L-MAD）框架，系统评估了不同辩论结构和聚合方法在法律文本蕴含中的表现。通过赋予多个智能体不同专家角色，L-MAD在强单智能体基线基础上性能提升了8%。研究还发现，增加智能体数量能减少矛盾、提升准确率，但延长辩论轮数会导致“过度审议漂移”，即智能体互相加深错误。本研究明确了在高风险法律环境中部署协作多智能体系统的实际边界和安全范围。
summary_en: This work introduces the Legal Multi-Agent Debate (L-MAD) framework to systematically evaluate various debate structures and aggregation methods in Legal Textual Entailment. By assigning distinct expert personas to multiple agents, L-MAD improves performance by up to 8% over strong single-agent baselines. The study reveals a trade-off: increasing the number of agents reduces inconsistency and boosts accuracy, while extending debate rounds causes an over-deliberation drift where agents reinforce each other's mistakes. The findings delineate practical limits and safety margins for deploying collaborative multi-agent systems in high-stakes legal reasoning.
why_it_matters_zh: 法律领域知识高度结构化且严谨，研究表明多智能体辩论能有效提升法律文本推理的准确性，同时揭示了辩论规模和轮数对系统性能的复杂影响，对实际应用多智能体系统具有重要指导意义。
why_it_matters_en: The legal domain demands precise and structured knowledge handling; this research demonstrates that multi-agent debate frameworks enhance accuracy in legal textual reasoning. Moreover, it uncovers nuanced effects of agent population and debate length on performance, offering critical insights for safely applying multi-agent systems in legal contexts.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: L-MAD: A Systematic Evaluation of Multi-Agent Debate Structures in Legal Reasoning. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: ProofCouncil：用于解决开放数学问题的大型语言模型代理
title_en: ProofCouncil: An LLM Agent for Solving Open Mathematical Problems
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）在解决开放数学问题方面显示出越来越大的潜力，但其性能可通过更贴近实际数学工作的代理流程进一步提升。为此，作者介绍了ProofCouncil，这是一种数学代理，采用作者-评论架构来解决开放问题。ProofCouncil作为FirstProof挑战赛第二批参赛作品，针对10个真实数学问题中，6个问题的提交结果被评审认为正确（最多需小修），表现最佳。它还在30个收集的开放数学问题中进行评估，在收到人类反馈的21个解决方案中，5个被认定完全正确，2个有望最终验证正确，8个提供了有用的部分进展。本文简要介绍了ProofCouncil的发展及所用的开源代理构建库。
summary_en: Large language models (LLMs) have shown promising capabilities in solving open mathematical problems, yet their performance can be enhanced via agentic workflows aligned with actual mathematical practice. ProofCouncil is introduced as a mathematical agent using an author-critic architecture to tackle these problems. It participated in the FirstProof challenge submitting solutions to 10 real-world problems; 6 submissions were judged correct with minor revisions, achieving the best among teams. Evaluated on 30 open problems from researchers, 5 solutions were fully correct, 2 promising, and 8 had useful partial progress. The paper outlines the development of ProofCouncil and releases the agent-building library as open source.
why_it_matters_zh: ProofCouncil通过结合作者-评论架构，实现了在开放数学问题上的领先表现，展示了大型语言模型与智能代理结合在复杂数学任务中的潜力。它在FirstProof挑战赛中的优异成绩，以及在真实研究问题上的多项有效进展，证明了该方法的实际价值和推广前景。公开代理构建库亦有助社区进一步研究。
why_it_matters_en: ProofCouncil demonstrates how integrating an author-critic architecture with large language models can effectively address open mathematical problems. Its top performance at the FirstProof challenge and multiple validated solutions to real research problems highlight its practical impact and potential for broader application. Moreover, releasing the agent-building library as open source facilitates ongoing community-driven advancement in AI-powered mathematical reasoning.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: ProofCouncil: An LLM Agent for Solving Open Mathematical Problems. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: HALO：用于语言模型的混合自适应潜在推理
title_en: HALO: Hybrid Adaptive Latent Reasoning for Language Models
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种名为HALO的混合自适应潜在精炼方法，用于提升冻结的预训练语言模型，在保持计算高效的同时改进推理能力。HALO通过粗略精炼阶段结合对部分关键令牌的选择性二次精炼，实现更合理的计算资源分配。在MMLU-Pro和GPQA-Diamond组成的公共基准测试中，HALO表现优于固定的单步或双步精炼方法，达成了更高的整体平均准确率，同时使用更少的计算步骤。
summary_en: This paper introduces HALO, a hybrid adaptive latent refinement method designed to improve frozen pretrained language models by adding a small amount of adaptive extra computation. HALO combines a coarse refinement stage with selective second-stage latent refinement on a subset of tokens chosen by token scoring and monotonic token halting. On major benchmarks built from MMLU-Pro and GPQA-Diamond, HALO outperforms fixed one-step and two-step refinement methods, achieving the best average accuracy while using fewer refinement steps and less computational overhead.
why_it_matters_zh: HALO的优势在于有效分配计算资源，通过智能选择需要二次精炼的令牌，既避免了过度计算，又保证了推理效果，从而提升语言模型的性能和效率。
why_it_matters_en: The key advantage of HALO lies in its intelligent allocation of refinement effort, selectively applying additional refinement only where needed. This reduces wasteful computation while maintaining or even improving accuracy, leading to more efficient and effective language model reasoning.

title_zh: AgentKGV：用于知识图谱事实验证的具代理性的LLM-RAG框架及两阶段训练
title_en: AgentKGV: Agentic LLM-RAG Framework with Two-Stage Training for the Fact Verification of Knowledge Graphs
summary_zh: 知识图谱通常自动构建自大规模语料，但因噪声源和提取失败常含事实错误，工业规模下可靠验证仍是难题。AgentKGV提出了一种集成动态路由与迭代查询重写的具代理性LLM-RAG框架，解决文档级检索中的表面形式不匹配问题。通过两阶段训练策略——基于回合蒸馏的微调和轨迹级GRPO优化搜索策略，实现更精确且高效的工业部署。在开域T-REx长尾谓词测试上，框架提升宏F1值5.5个百分点，两阶段训练提升9.4个百分点，且GRPO将搜索调用次数从3.24降至1.63，准确率无降低。
summary_en: Knowledge graphs constructed from large corpora often contain factual errors. AgentKGV presents an agentic LLM-RAG framework integrating dynamic routing and iterative query rewriting to address surface-form mismatches in document-level retrieval. It employs a two-stage training strategy with turn-level distillation-based supervised fine-tuning and trajectory-level GRPO to optimize retrieval efficiency and accuracy. On the open-domain T-REx benchmark long-tail predicates, it improves macro-F1 by 5.5% over single-turn RAG, further enhanced to 9.4% with two-stage training. GRPO reduces average search calls from 3.24 to 1.63 without accuracy loss.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究显著提升了知识图谱自动验证的准确性和效率，解决了工业部署中检索匹配和计算成本的问题，对提升知识图谱质量和应用具有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: This work significantly improves the accuracy and efficiency of automated fact verification in knowledge graphs, addressing retrieval mismatch and computational cost challenges in industrial deployment, thus enhancing knowledge graph quality and usability.

title_zh: 面向灵敏度的阈值设定与令牌路由用于大型语言模型的激活稀疏化
title_en: Sensitivity-Aware Thresholding and Token Routing for Activation Sparsification in Large Language Models
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种用于大型语言模型（LLMs）推理加速的新方法。通过多层感知机（MLP）激活稀疏化和令牌级条件路由，作者提出了灵敏度感知阈值设定（SATS），利用局部MLP输出灵敏度来校准层级阈值，替代传统依赖激活百分位的阈值设置方法。同时，引入了轻量级令牌路由框架，动态选择基础路径或修改路径以优化单个令牌的计算。实验证明，SATS在相似稀疏度下优于基于阈值的稀疏化基线，令牌路由则实现了更优的质量与吞吐量权衡。
summary_en: This paper introduces novel methods for accelerating inference in Large Language Models (LLMs) using MLP activation sparsification and token-level conditional routing. The proposed Sensitivity-Aware Thresholding for Sparsity (SATS) calibrates layer-wise thresholds based on local MLP output sensitivity instead of activation percentiles. Additionally, a lightweight token routing framework dynamically selects computation paths per token. Evaluations on recent open-weight LLMs show SATS improves over threshold-based baselines at matched sparsity, and token routing offers better quality-throughput trade-offs.
why_it_matters_zh: LLMs推理效率关键在于合理减少计算量同时保障模型质量。传统基于激活百分位的稀疏化阈值设定未必反映局部敏感度，影响分类质量。本文通过灵敏度感知阈值设定和令牌路由策略，有效提升了激活稀疏化效果和推理性能，对于追求高效大模型部署具有实际意义。
why_it_matters_en: Efficient LLM inference depends on reducing computation without compromising quality. Conventional percentile-based sparsity thresholds may not capture local output sensitivity, limiting effectiveness. By adopting sensitivity-aware thresholding and token-level routing, this work enhances activation sparsification and inference speed, providing practical benefits for deploying large models efficiently.

title_zh: CogniConsole：将推理时控制外部化作为可靠大型语言模型交互的形式抽象
title_en: CogniConsole: Externalizing Inference-Time Control as a Formal Abstraction for Reliable LLM Interactions
summary_zh: 本文提出CogniConsole架构，将推理时控制外部化为结构化接口，结合程序化协调和有界提示推理，显著提升大型语言模型（LLM）系统的可靠性。通过489个多步骤互动探查，展示增加结构化脚手架能系统性降低输出方差和失败率，表明失败模式多因控制不足而非能力不足。
summary_en: This work introduces CogniConsole, an architecture that externalizes inference-time control into a structured interface combining programmatic coordination with bounded prompt-based reasoning. Experiments with 489 multi-step interactive probes demonstrate that adding structural scaffolding systematically reduces output variance and failure rates, indicating that many failures stem from under-specified control rather than lack of model capability.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究突破传统将可靠性归因于模型能力的观点，强调推理时控制的重要性，为LLM设计和评估提供新方向，促进更稳定可信的交互体验。
why_it_matters_en: It challenges the conventional focus on model capability by highlighting inference-time control's critical role, providing a new paradigm for designing and evaluating LLM systems that emphasizes stability and reliability beyond mere scaling.

title_zh: GATS：结合分层世界模型的图增强树搜索实现高效智能体规划
title_en: GATS: Graph-Augmented Tree Search with Layered World Models for Efficient Agent Planning
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）智能体在多步骤规划任务中表现出潜力，但现有方法如LATS和ReAct在规划过程中高度依赖LLM推理，导致计算成本高且行为具有随机性。本文提出GATS（图增强树搜索），结合基于UCB1的系统搜索和分层世界模型，消除推理期间的LLM调用，同时实现更优规划性能。分层世界模型包括精确符号动作匹配、执行日志学习统计和基于LLM的未知动作预测。在合成规划任务和12个挑战场景中，GATS均实现100%成功率，显著优于LATS和ReAct，且零LLM调用、规划结果确定无方差。结果表明系统搜索结合学习世界模型，在智能体规划中大幅优于LLM引导探索。
summary_en: Large Language Model (LLM) agents show promise in multi-step planning, but existing methods like LATS and ReAct rely heavily on LLM inference during planning, resulting in high computational cost and stochastic behavior. This paper introduces GATS (Graph-Augmented Tree Search), a framework combining systematic UCB1-based tree search with a layered world model to eliminate LLM calls during inference while achieving superior planning performance. The three-layer model integrates exact symbolic action matching, statistics learned from execution logs, and LLM-based prediction for unknown actions. On synthetic tasks and a diverse set of 12 challenging scenarios, GATS achieves 100% success rate, outperforming LATS and ReAct, requires zero LLM calls per task, and produces deterministic plans with zero variance. These results demonstrate that systematic search combined with learned world models can substantially surpass LLM-guided exploration for agent planning.
why_it_matters_zh: GATS通过引入分层世界模型，有效减少了对昂贵且不稳定的LLM推理的依赖，实现在规划时零次LLM调用，同时利用系统性树搜索提升了规划成功率和确定性，极大提升了智能体的效率与表现。
why_it_matters_en: By integrating a layered world model, GATS effectively reduces reliance on costly and stochastic LLM inference, enabling zero LLM calls during planning. Its systematic tree search improves success rates and determinism, greatly enhancing agent efficiency and performance.
